---
title: "Spectral signature"
format: html
editor: visual
---

---
title: "Spectral signature_june_23_2025"
output: html_document
author: "Polycarpe Mizero" 
date: "June 23 2025"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    theme: cerulean
---

# Set up

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#install.packages("hms")
#install.packages("fluxfinder")
#install.packages("goflux")
#install.packages("remotes")
#remotes::install_github("COMPASS-DOE/fluxfinder", build_vignettes = TRUE)
#install.packages("fuzzyjoin")
#install.packages("tidyverse")

library(readxl) # to read excel files
library(janitor) # to clean data
library(dplyr) # wrangling
library(tidyr) # wrangling
library(readr) # to export csv
library(hms)
library(ggplot2)
library(fuzzyjoin)
library(lme4)
library(car)
library(patchwork)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(emmeans)
library(multcompView)
library(fs)
library(purrr)
library(fs)
library(stringr)
library(broom)
library(tidymodels)


```

## The general plan

load all 60 spectral .sed files plot individual spectral signatures combine them for overall plots. Use random forest classification model to identify or classify surface types.

Plot

Individual reflectance curves.

Then overlay all 60.

```{r data cleaning}



# Define the path to your .sed file
file_path <- "../data/20250623/SR-3500_SN20880T4_00000.sed"  # actual path

# Read the file, skipping the first 26 lines
gh <- read_tsv(
  file = file_path,
  skip = 27,
  col_names = c("wavelength", "radiance_ref", "radiance_target", "reflectance_percent")
)

# View the first few rows
head(gh)


```

# Data wrangling

```{r}
gh_1 <- clean_names(gh)

head(gh_1)
```

```{r select necessary columns}
gh_w <- gh_1 %>%
  dplyr::select(wave_length = wavelength, reflectance_percent)

head(gh_w)
```

```{r}
summary(gh_w)
```

```{r}
glimpse(gh_w)
```

```{r single_plot}
ggplot(data = gh_w,
       aes(x = wave_length,
           y = reflectance_percent)) + 
  geom_line(color="darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Reflectance variation",
       x = "Wave length (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)") +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Load all 60 files into one dataframe

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# Set folder path
folder_path <- "../data/20250623/"  

# Extract date from folder name and format it
date_value <- basename(folder_path) %>%
  str_remove("/") %>%
  as.Date(format = "%Y%m%d") %>%
  format("%Y_%m_%d")

# List all .sed files
sed_files <- dir_ls(folder_path, glob = "*.sed")

# Define function to read and process one .sed file
read_sed_file <- function(file_path) {
  # Extract file code (last two digits before .sed)
  file_code <- str_extract(basename(file_path), "(\\d{2})(?=\\.sed$)")

  # Read the file and add file_code column
  read_tsv(
    file = file_path,
    skip = 27,
    col_names = c("wave_length", "radiance_ref", "radiance_target", "reflectance_percent"),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  ) %>%
    mutate(file_code = as.numeric(file_code),
           date = date_value
           )
}

# Apply the function to all files and combine
all_spectra <- map_dfr(sed_files, read_sed_file)

# View the combined data
head(all_spectra)

#all_spectra %>%
# filter(file_code == 60)


```

```{r}


# Read Excel mapping
mapping <- read_excel("../data/file names/june_23_2025.xlsx") %>%
  rename(plot_number = `Plot number`,
         rep = Rep,
         treatment = `Treatment name`,
         file_code = `File name`) %>%
  mutate(treatment = str_replace_all(treatment, "\\+", "_"))


# Join mapping with files
matched_files <- all_spectra %>%
  inner_join(mapping, by = "file_code") %>%
  mutate(sample_id = paste0(treatment, "_", plot_number))


head(matched_files)


#matched_files %>%
 # filter(file_code == 60)
```

## Extract treatment info from filenames

```{r}
#spliting the treatment column into forage and treatment
spectra_data <- matched_files %>%
  separate(treatment, into = c("forage", "treatment"), sep = "_")%>%
  dplyr::select(date, sample_id, plot_number, wave_length, reflectance_percent, rep, forage, treatment)

head(spectra_data)
```

## abbreviations to full names

```{r}
spectra_data <- spectra_data %>%
  mutate(
    forage = case_when(
      forage == "be" ~ "bermudagrass",
      forage == "al" ~ "alfalfa",
      forage == "cra" ~ "crabgrass",
      forage == "un" ~ "unplanted",
      TRUE ~ forage   # keep original if not matched
    ),
    treatment = case_when(
      treatment == "U" ~ "urine",
      treatment == "D" ~ "dung",
      treatment == "cont" ~ "control",
      TRUE ~ treatment   # keep original if not matched
    )
  )

spectra_data
```

```{r}
summary(spectra_data$wave_length)
```

```{r}
glimpse(spectra_data)
```

```{r}
write.csv(spectra_data, "../output/spectral_data_june_23.csv")
```

```{r}
Spectra_gitelson <- spectra_data %>%
 filter(wave_length <= 1450) 

Spectra_gitelson
```

```{r}
write.csv(Spectra_gitelson, "../output/Spectra_gitelson_june_23.csv")
```

## Removing treatments with bias data

```{r}

# Vector of sample_ids to remove
#remove_ids <- c("cra_cont_102", "cra_D_109", "cra_U_111", "cra_D_209", 
 #              "al_D_211", "al_D_312", "cra_D_402", "al_D_412", 
  #              "cra_U_501", "al_D_510", "al_U_511")

# Remove those rows
#spectra_data <- spectra_data %>%
 # filter(!sample_id %in% remove_ids)

# Check dimensions before/after
#dim(spectra_data)
#dim(spectra_data)

# Preview cleaned data
#head(spectra_data)

```

## Plot individual spectral signatures

```{r}
spectra_data %>% 
  filter(sample_id == "be_U_112") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent)) +
  geom_line(color="darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Spectral signature for un_u_101") +
  theme_minimal()

```

## removong water bands that are prroblematic

```{r}
spectra_data %>% 
  filter(
    sample_id == "be_U_112",
    wave_length <= 1100
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Spectral signature for un_u_101") +
  theme_minimal()

```

## plot for small multiples

```{r}
spectra_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, color = forage)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~sample_id) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Spectral variation for all treatments",
       x = "Wave length (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%))",
       fill = "Forage species")

```

## combined plot of all samples

```{r}
ggplot(spectra_data, aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, group = sample_id, color = treatment)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  labs(title = "Spectral signatures for all samples",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)") +
  theme_minimal()

```

##removing water bands that are problematic

```{r}
spectra_data %>%
 filter(wave_length <= 1450) %>% 
ggplot(aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, group = sample_id, color = treatment)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  labs(title = "Spectral signatures for all samples",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)") +
  theme_minimal() 
  
```

Spectral curves show the classic vegetation signature: low reflectance in the red (around 670 nm) due to chlorophyll absorption, sharp rise at the red edge (\~700 nm), and plateau in NIR (800-1100 nm).

```{r}
ggsave("../output/all_spectral_june_23.png",
       width = 6,
       height = 4
       )
```

## combined plot by forage species

```{r}
ggplot(spectra_data, aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, group = sample_id, color = treatment)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  facet_wrap(~forage) +
  labs(title = "Spectral signatures for all samples",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length <= 1450) %>% 
ggplot(aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, group = sample_id, color = treatment)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  facet_wrap(~forage) +
  labs(title = "Spectral signatures for all forage samples",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggsave("../output/spectral_by_forage_june_23.png",
       width = 6,
       height = 4
       )
```

## combined plot by treatments applied

```{r}
ggplot(spectra_data, aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, group = sample_id, color = forage)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  facet_wrap(~treatment) +
  labs(title = "Spectral signatures for all treatment samples",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length <= 1450) %>% 
ggplot(aes(x = wave_length, y = reflectance_percent, group = sample_id, color = forage)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  facet_wrap(~treatment) +
  labs(title = "Spectral signatures for all treatment samples",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance (%)",
       fill = "Forage species") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggsave("../output/spectral_by_treatment_june_23.png",
       width = 6,
       height = 4
       )
```

## plotting the SD within and across treatments

```{r}


# 1. Calculate within-treatment standard deviation at each wavelength
within_sd <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length <= 1450)%>%
  group_by(treatment, wave_length) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_reflectance = mean(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_reflectance = sd(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2. Calculate across-treatment separation (range of means)
across_sep <- within_sd %>%
  group_by(wave_length) %>%
  summarise(
    range_means = max(mean_reflectance, na.rm = TRUE) -
                  min(mean_reflectance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. Plot within-treatment variability (example: for each treatment)
ggplot(within_sd, aes(x = wave_length, y = sd_reflectance, color = treatment)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Within-treatment variability of spectral reflectance",
    x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Standard Deviation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 4. Plot across-treatment separation
ggplot(across_sep, aes(x = wave_length, y = range_means)) +
  geom_line(color = "black", size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Across-treatment spectral separation",
    x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Range of mean reflectance across treatments"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 5. Optional: Combine into one figure (using facets)
combined <- within_sd %>%
  group_by(wave_length) %>%
  summarise(
    across_range = max(mean_reflectance, na.rm = TRUE) -
                   min(mean_reflectance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(within_sd, by = "wave_length")

ggplot(combined, aes(x = wave_length)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = sd_reflectance, color = treatment), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(y = across_range), color = "black", size = 1.2, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Within- and Across-treatment Spectral Variation",
    x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Reflectance variability"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Compute across-treatment separation again
across_sep <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length <= 1450)%>%
  group_by(treatment, wave_length) %>%
  summarise(mean_reflectance = mean(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(wave_length) %>%
  summarise(range_means = max(mean_reflectance, na.rm = TRUE) -
                           min(mean_reflectance, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

# 2. Define threshold (e.g., top 5% of separations)
threshold <- quantile(across_sep$range_means, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)

peak_regions <- across_sep %>%
  filter(range_means >= threshold) %>%
  arrange(wave_length)

# 3. Collapse consecutive wavelengths into ranges
peak_ranges <- peak_regions %>%
  mutate(group = cumsum(c(1, diff(wave_length) > 5))) %>%   # 5 nm gap = new range
  group_by(group) %>%
  summarise(start_wl = min(wave_length),
            end_wl   = max(wave_length),
            max_sep  = max(range_means),
            .groups = "drop")

print(peak_ranges)

# 4. Plot with shaded bands for ranges
ggplot(across_sep, aes(x = wave_length, y = range_means)) +
  geom_line(color = "black", size = 1) +
  geom_rect(data = peak_ranges,
            aes(xmin = start_wl, xmax = end_wl,
                ymin = 0, ymax = max_sep),
            fill = "red", alpha = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Across-treatment spectral separation (highlighting key wavelength ranges)",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Range of mean reflectance") +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Calculating vegetation indices

NDVI: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index NDRE: Normalized Difference Red Edge EVI: Enhanced Vegetation Index \# measures vegetation "greenness" and vigor SAVI: Soil Adjusted Vegetation Index \# minimize the influence of soil brightness when assessing plant health.

We can make multiple vegetation indices with different combinations of different bands.

# Extract all wavelengths (Band names) under each Band

NDVI = (NIR_Band - Red_Band) / (NIR_Band + Red_Band)

Wavelength starts from 350 nm to 2500 nm

Blue: wave_length between 450–495 nm

Red: wave_length between 630–690 nm: 61 Bands

Red_edge: wave_length between 680-750 nm:

NIR: wave_length between 700–1000 nm: 301 bands

For example with that, we can calculate a total of 60 x 300 = **18000** different NDVIs!

Lets extract the Bands name which falls under Red and NIR Bands for this sensor, and calculate NDVI.

## Filter for red and NIR bands

```{r}
# Filter the necessary bands
spectra_blue <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 450, wave_length <= 495) %>%
  rename(blue_wl = wave_length, blue_val = reflectance_percent)

spectra_blue

spectra_red <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 630, wave_length <= 690) %>%
  rename(red_wl = wave_length, red_val = reflectance_percent)

spectra_red

spectra_redge <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 680, wave_length <= 750) %>%
  rename(redge_wl = wave_length, redge_val = reflectance_percent)

spectra_redge

spectra_nir <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 700, wave_length <= 1000) %>%
  rename(nir_wl = wave_length, nir_val = reflectance_percent)

spectra_nir
```

## all bands combination and calculate all vegetation indices

Pivot the dataset to a long format so each wavelength is a column. Generate all possible red–NIR wavelength pairings

Calculate vegatation indices for each pair using:

NDVI = (NIR - Red) / (NIR + Red) NDRE = (NIR – RedEdge) / (NIR + RedEdge) EVI = 2.5 × (NIR – Red) / (NIR + 6×Red – 7.5×Blue + 1) SAVI = 1.5 \* (nir - red) / (nir + red + 0.5)

```{r}

library(dplyr)

# Collapse each band range to mean reflectance per sample_id
spectra_blue <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 450, wave_length <= 495) %>%
  group_by(sample_id, forage, treatment) %>%
  summarise(blue_val = mean(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
            blue_wl = mean(wave_length), .groups = "drop")

spectra_red <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 630, wave_length <= 690) %>%
  group_by(sample_id, forage, treatment) %>%
  summarise(red_val = mean(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
            red_wl = mean(wave_length), .groups = "drop")

spectra_redge <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 680, wave_length <= 750) %>%
  group_by(sample_id, forage, treatment) %>%
  summarise(redge_val = mean(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
            redge_wl = mean(wave_length), .groups = "drop")

spectra_nir <- spectra_data %>%
  filter(wave_length >= 700, wave_length <= 1000) %>%
  group_by(sample_id, forage, treatment) %>%
  summarise(nir_val = mean(reflectance_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
            nir_wl = mean(wave_length), .groups = "drop")

# ✅ Now join safely (1 row per sample_id per band)
spectra_all <- spectra_red %>%
  inner_join(spectra_nir, by = c("sample_id", "forage", "treatment")) %>%
  inner_join(spectra_redge, by = c("sample_id", "forage", "treatment")) %>%
  inner_join(spectra_blue, by = c("sample_id", "forage", "treatment")) %>%
  mutate(
    # NDVI
    ndvi = (nir_val - red_val) / (nir_val + red_val),
    # NDRE
    ndre = (nir_val - redge_val) / (nir_val + redge_val),
    # EVI
    evi = 2.5 * (nir_val - red_val) / (nir_val + 6 * red_val - 7.5 * blue_val + 1)
  ) %>%
  select(sample_id, forage, treatment,
         red_wl, nir_wl, redge_wl, blue_wl,
         ndvi, ndre, evi)

# Preview results
head(spectra_all)

```

I wanted every red × NIR within the same sample. The results give a long dataframe with sample-wise full band combinations. The results shows all NDVI combinations for each sample. Each row tells which red and NIR bands were used.

```{r}
summary(spectra_all$ndvi)
```

```{r}

spectra_all_clean <- spectra_data %>%
  mutate(surface_type = paste(forage, treatment, sep = "_"))

# View the updated data
head(spectra_all_clean)

```

```{r}
write.csv(spectra_all_clean, "../data/all_dates/june23.csv", row.names = TRUE)
```

```{r}
spectra_data_cleann <- spectra_all_clean %>%
  mutate(surface_type = paste(forage, treatment, sep = "_"))

# View the updated data
head(spectra_data_cleann)
```

##RF classification model

Load and preprocess the data. Split into training (70%) and testing (30%). Build a random forest classification model with tuning on mtry, trees, and min_n. Fit the tuned model. Evaluate performance using confusion matrix and accuracy.

## Random forest should be trained on a wide dataset (one row per sample, multiple predictors = reflectance/indices), not a long dataset (many rows per sample).

## Pivoting wider the dataset

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Convert long -> wide format
spectra_wide <- spectra_data_cleann %>%
  select(sample_id, treatment, surface_type, wave_length, reflectance_percent) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = wave_length,
    values_from = reflectance_percent,
    names_prefix = "wl_"
  )%>%
  mutate(treatment = as.factor(treatment))

# Remove sample_id if not needed, keep surface_type as label
#spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  #select(-sample_id) 

spectra_wide
```

Each row = one sample, each column = one reflectance feature. This massively reduces training time.

## Ensure consistent factor levels before split

```{r}
# Make sure treatment is factor and has all levels
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment)) %>%
  mutate(surface_type = factor(surface_type))%>%
  droplevels()

spectra_wide
```

## amendment treatment ()

```{r}
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  select(-sample_id) %>%
  select(-surface_type)

spectra_wide
```

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Clean data: remove Inf/-Inf/NA
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(., Inf))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(., -Inf))) %>%
  drop_na()

# 2. Split (make sure factor levels match between train/test)
set.seed(123)
data_split <- initial_split(spectra_wide, prop = 0.7, strata = treatment)
train_data <- training(data_split) %>%
  mutate(treatment = droplevels(treatment))
test_data <- testing(data_split) %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, levels = levels(train_data$treatment)))

# 3. Recipe with PCA (safe now)
rf_recipe <- recipe(treatment ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), num_comp = 20)

# 4. Random forest model
rf_model <- rand_forest(mtry = tune(), trees = tune(), min_n = tune()) %>%
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("ranger")

rf_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(rf_recipe) %>%
  add_model(rf_model)

# 5. Cross-validation folds
set.seed(123)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(train_data, v = 3, strata = treatment)

# 6. Tuning grid (light for speed)
rf_grid <- grid_random(
  mtry(range = c(2, 20)),
  trees(range = c(200, 500)),
  min_n(range = c(2, 10)),
  size = 10
)

# 7. Tune
rf_tuned <- tune_grid(
  rf_workflow,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  grid = rf_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy),
  control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE)
)

# 8. Best model
best_rf <- select_best(rf_tuned, metric = "accuracy")
final_rf <- finalize_workflow(rf_workflow, best_rf)

# 9. Fit final model
rf_fit <- fit(final_rf, data = train_data)

# 10. Evaluate
rf_results <- predict(rf_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  metrics(truth = treatment, estimate = .pred_class)

rf_conf_mat <- predict(rf_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  conf_mat(truth = treatment, estimate = .pred_class)

rf_results
rf_conf_mat


```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Get a plain contingency table from whatever you have
cm_tbl <- if (inherits(rf_conf_mat, "conf_mat")) rf_conf_mat$table else rf_conf_mat

# 2) Convert to a tidy data frame: Truth, Prediction, n
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_tbl)

# Handle common column name variants
if (all(c("Var1","Var2","Freq") %in% names(cm_df))) {
  names(cm_df)[1:3] <- c("Truth","Prediction","n")
} else if (all(c("Reference","Prediction","Freq") %in% names(cm_df))) {
  cm_df <- cm_df %>% rename(Truth = Reference, n = Freq)
} else if ("Freq" %in% names(cm_df) && !"n" %in% names(cm_df)) {
  cm_df <- cm_df %>% rename(n = Freq)
}

# Ensure factors, order rows top-to-bottom
cm_df <- cm_df %>%
  mutate(
    Truth = factor(Truth, levels = rev(unique(Truth))),
    Prediction = factor(Prediction, levels = unique(Prediction))
  )


# 3) Heatmap of counts with vertical x-axis labels
p_counts <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Prediction, y = Truth, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix (Counts)", 
       x = "Predicted", y = "True", fill = "Count")

print(p_counts)

# 4) Heatmap of row-wise percentages with vertical x-axis labels
cm_pct <- cm_df %>%
  group_by(Truth) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

p_pct <- ggplot(cm_pct, aes(x = Prediction, y = Truth, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix (Row %)", 
       x = "Predicted", y = "True", fill = "Percent")

print(p_pct)


```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "../output/confusion_matrix_june_23.png",
  plot = p_counts,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
```

#GITELSON approach

To increase accuracy of surface type classification: wave-length range λ from 520 to 585 nm and from 695 to 740 nm

```{r}

spectra_subset <- spectra_data_cleann %>%
  filter((wave_length >= 680 & wave_length <= 750) |
         (wave_length >= 750 & wave_length <= 1000)) 

spectra_subset

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Convert long -> wide format
spectra_wide <- spectra_subset %>%
  select(sample_id, treatment, surface_type, wave_length, reflectance_percent) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = wave_length,
    values_from = reflectance_percent,
    names_prefix = "wl_"
  )%>%
  mutate(treatment = as.factor(treatment))

# Remove sample_id if not needed, keep surface_type as label
#spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  #select(-sample_id) 

spectra_wide
```

Each row = one sample, each column = one reflectance feature. This massively reduces training time.

## Ensure consistent factor levels before split

```{r}
# Make sure treatment is factor and has all levels
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment)) %>%
  mutate(surface_type = factor(surface_type))%>%
  droplevels()

spectra_wide
```

## amendment treatment ()

```{r}
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  select(-sample_id) %>%
  select(-surface_type)

spectra_wide
```

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Clean data: remove Inf/-Inf/NA
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(., Inf))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(., -Inf))) %>%
  drop_na()

# 2. Split (make sure factor levels match between train/test)
set.seed(123)
data_split <- initial_split(spectra_wide, prop = 0.7, strata = treatment)
train_data <- training(data_split) %>%
  mutate(treatment = droplevels(treatment))
test_data <- testing(data_split) %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, levels = levels(train_data$treatment)))

# 3. Recipe with PCA (safe now)
rf_recipe <- recipe(treatment ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), num_comp = 20)

# 4. Random forest model
rf_model <- rand_forest(mtry = tune(), trees = tune(), min_n = tune()) %>%
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("ranger")

rf_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(rf_recipe) %>%
  add_model(rf_model)

# 5. Cross-validation folds
set.seed(123)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(train_data, v = 3, strata = treatment)

# 6. Tuning grid (light for speed)
rf_grid <- grid_random(
  mtry(range = c(2, 20)),
  trees(range = c(200, 500)),
  min_n(range = c(2, 10)),
  size = 10
)

# 7. Tune
rf_tuned <- tune_grid(
  rf_workflow,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  grid = rf_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy),
  control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE)
)

# 8. Best model
best_rf <- select_best(rf_tuned, metric = "accuracy")
final_rf <- finalize_workflow(rf_workflow, best_rf)

# 9. Fit final model
rf_fit <- fit(final_rf, data = train_data)

# 10. Evaluate
rf_results <- predict(rf_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  metrics(truth = treatment, estimate = .pred_class)

rf_conf_mat <- predict(rf_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  conf_mat(truth = treatment, estimate = .pred_class)

rf_results
rf_conf_mat


```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Get a plain contingency table from whatever you have
cm_tbl <- if (inherits(rf_conf_mat, "conf_mat")) rf_conf_mat$table else rf_conf_mat

# 2) Convert to a tidy data frame: Truth, Prediction, n
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_tbl)

# Handle common column name variants
if (all(c("Var1","Var2","Freq") %in% names(cm_df))) {
  names(cm_df)[1:3] <- c("Truth","Prediction","n")
} else if (all(c("Reference","Prediction","Freq") %in% names(cm_df))) {
  cm_df <- cm_df %>% rename(Truth = Reference, n = Freq)
} else if ("Freq" %in% names(cm_df) && !"n" %in% names(cm_df)) {
  cm_df <- cm_df %>% rename(n = Freq)
}

# Ensure factors, order rows top-to-bottom
cm_df <- cm_df %>%
  mutate(
    Truth = factor(Truth, levels = rev(unique(Truth))),
    Prediction = factor(Prediction, levels = unique(Prediction))
  )


# 3) Heatmap of counts with vertical x-axis labels
p_counts <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Prediction, y = Truth, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix (Counts)", 
       x = "Predicted", y = "True", fill = "Count")

print(p_counts)

# 4) Heatmap of row-wise percentages with vertical x-axis labels
cm_pct <- cm_df %>%
  group_by(Truth) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

p_pct <- ggplot(cm_pct, aes(x = Prediction, y = Truth, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix (Row %)", 
       x = "Predicted", y = "True", fill = "Percent")

print(p_pct)


```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "../output/improved_confusion_matrix_june_23.png",
  plot = p_counts,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
```

# improving accuracy

Accuracy can be improved by adding more spectral features, using dimensionality reduction (PCA), and trying gradient boosting (XGBoost)

Workflow: Pre-processing (indices + PCA on reflectance) Feature engineering (NDVI, NDRE, EVI, SAVI) Model training (Random Forest + XGBoost) Hyperparameter tuning Evaluation (accuracy, kappa, confusion matrix, heatmap)

```{r}
#install.packages("themis")
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Libraries
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
library(tidymodels)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(vip)          # variable importance
library(xgboost)      # boosted trees
library(themis)       # class balancing (SMOTE if needed)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Prepare dataset
# Assume `spectra_data_clean` has sample_id, surface_type, wave_length, reflectance_percent
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Select important bands
spectra_wide <- spectra_data_clean %>%
  filter(wave_length %in% c(450, 660, 720, 800)) %>%  # blue, red, rededge, nir (example)
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = wave_length,
    values_from = reflectance_percent,
    names_prefix = "wl_"
  )

# Rename for clarity
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  rename(
    blue = wl_450,
    red = wl_660,
    rededge = wl_720,
    nir = wl_800
  )

# Add vegetation indices
spectra_wide <- spectra_wide %>%
  mutate(
    NDVI = (nir - red) / (nir + red),
    NDRE = (nir - rededge) / (nir + rededge),
    EVI  = 2.5 * (nir - red) / (nir + 6*red - 7.5*blue + 1),
    SAVI = 1.5 * (nir - red) / (nir + red + 0.5)
  ) %>%
  mutate(surface_type = as.factor(surface_type)) %>%
  distinct(sample_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Train-test split
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(123)
data_split <- initial_split(spectra_wide, prop = 0.7, strata = surface_type)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data  <- testing(data_split)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Preprocessing recipe
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rf_recipe <- recipe(surface_type ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  update_role(sample_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), num_comp = 20) # reduce dimensionality

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Model specifications
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rf_model <- rand_forest(
  mtry = tune(),
  trees = tune(),
  min_n = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger") %>%
  set_mode("classification")

xgb_model <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune(),
  tree_depth = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  loss_reduction = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>%
  set_mode("classification")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Workflows
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rf_wf  <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(rf_recipe) %>% add_model(rf_model)
xgb_wf <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(rf_recipe) %>% add_model(xgb_model)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Cross-validation
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(123)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(train_data, v = 5, strata = surface_type)

# Random grid for tuning
rf_grid <- grid_random(
  finalize(mtry(), train_data %>% select(-surface_type)),
  trees(range = c(200, 1000)),
  min_n(range = c(2, 20)),
  size = 15
)

xgb_grid <- grid_random(
  trees(range = c(200, 1000)),
  learn_rate(range = c(0.01, 0.3)),
  tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),
  min_n(range = c(2, 20)),
  loss_reduction(),
  size = 20
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7: Tune models
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rf_tuned <- tune_grid(
  rf_wf,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  grid = rf_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy, kap)
)

xgb_tuned <- tune_grid(
  xgb_wf,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  grid = xgb_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy, kap)
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 8: Select best models
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
best_rf  <- select_best(rf_tuned, metric = "accuracy")
best_xgb <- select_best(xgb_tuned, metric = "accuracy")

final_rf  <- finalize_workflow(rf_wf, best_rf)
final_xgb <- finalize_workflow(xgb_wf, best_xgb)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 9: Fit and Evaluate
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rf_fit  <- fit(final_rf, data = train_data)
xgb_fit <- fit(final_xgb, data = train_data)

rf_results <- predict(rf_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  metrics(truth = surface_type, estimate = .pred_class)

xgb_results <- predict(xgb_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  metrics(truth = surface_type, estimate = .pred_class)

# Confusion matrices
rf_conf <- predict(rf_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  conf_mat(truth = surface_type, estimate = .pred_class)

xgb_conf <- predict(xgb_fit, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data) %>%
  conf_mat(truth = surface_type, estimate = .pred_class)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 10: Plot Confusion Matrix (heatmap for RF)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
library(ggplot2)

rf_conf %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Prediction, y = Truth, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Random Forest Confusion Matrix")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 11: Compare model performance
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rf_results
xgb_results

```




```{r}

```
